copy.title: Tigers demolish Rays. White Sox shut out Twins. Knicks fight back tonight.
copy.keyTakeaway: Detroit, Chicago, and New York all crushed their opponents in dominant baseball performances. Tech stocks took a beating today—NVDA and MSFT both down over 3%—while crypto bled and bonds climbed. PlayStation's State of Play drops this week, and the NBA Finals resume tomorrow in San Antonio.

todaysHits.sports: Tigers, White Sox, Mets all dominant. Finals Game 2 tomorrow.
todaysHits.golf: Brian Campbell leads Memorial Tournament at even.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup opens June 11. Nine days out.
todaysHits.culture: PlayStation State of Play reveals 2026 lineup. DCU Superman test screenings perfect.

lead.headline: White Sox shut out Twins completely
lead.whatHappened: Chicago walked into Minnesota and blanked them 8-0. The Twins' offense just never showed up, and the White Sox pitching staff was untouchable.
lead.whyBullet1: Minnesota can't recover from a game where they scored nothing — that's not a bad night, that's a system failure.
lead.whyBullet2: The White Sox proved they can travel and dominate, not just win at home.
lead.whatToSay: The Twins looked like they weren't even trying to win that game.

markets.mood: Tech got hammered today—NVDA down 3.6% and MSFT down 3.2%—as the 10-year yield climbed 0.8%, making bonds suddenly look less terrible than they did last week.
markets.whyBullet1: If you're heavy in growth stocks inside your 401k, you're feeling this: rising rates make future earnings worth less in today's dollars, so the market reprices everything downward.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch Thursday and Friday for any Fed commentary or economic data that signals whether rates are staying elevated—if inflation data comes in hot, yields could spike higher and take tech down again.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin got crushed 4.1% today, worse than the broad market, which tells you risk-off sentiment is real right now and retail traders are bailing first.

culture.topic: PlayStation State of Play drops June news
culture.whatHappened: Sony held a State of Play showcase in June 2026 with new game announcements and trailers.
culture.whyItMatters: If you care what's coming to PS5 in the next year, this is where Sony shows their hand before E3 season gets crazy.
culture.whatToSay: Did you catch the State of Play? Some solid stuff coming down the pipe.

culture.topic: Steelers lock up Nick Herbig long term
culture.whatHappened: Pittsburgh signed edge rusher Nick Herbig to a lucrative multi-year extension.
culture.whyItMatters: The Steelers are betting on Herbig as a cornerstone pass rusher, which means they see him as part of their future defense and aren't shopping for edge help in free agency.
culture.whatToSay: Steelers finally committed to keeping Herbig around—guy's been productive and they're not messing with it.

culture.whatHappened: Ryan Coogler's supernatural horror film set in 1930s Mississippi Delta.
